source.title: Agentic AI Engineer — Senior Consultant / Consultant

digest: Retrieved, not guessed: 6 competency clusters, 26 concept/tool nodes grounded in real sources (24 Wikipedia/GitHub definitions), and 8 skills from the ESCO taxonomy — off-domain hits filtered out. Every node carries a real definition; edges are real relationships where an open KG had them.

graph.title: Agentic AI Engineer — Senior Consultant / Consultant

nodes.name: Agentic AI Engineer — Senior Consultant / Consultant
nodes.summary: The role, distilled from its posting.

nodes.name: Production SWE
nodes.summary: Ship production backend/services in Python (and a second language).

nodes.name: Software engineering
nodes.summary: Software engineering is a branch of both computer science and engineering focused on designing, developing, testing, and maintaining software applications (Wikipedia)

nodes.name: API
nodes.summary: An application programming interface (API) is a connection between computers or between computer programs (Wikipedia)

nodes.name: Distributed computing
nodes.summary: Distributed computing is a field of computer science that studies distributed systems, defined as computer systems whose inter-communicating components are located on different networked computers. (Wikipedia)

nodes.name: Enterprise / hybrid deployment
nodes.summary: Deploy inside the customer's environment under real access/security constraints.

nodes.name: Kubernetes
nodes.summary: Kubernetes, also known as K8s, is an open-source container orchestration system for automating software deployment, scaling, and management (Wikipedia)

nodes.name: Role-based access control
nodes.summary: In information security, role-based access control (RBAC) or role-based security is an approach to restricting system access to authorized users (Wikipedia)

nodes.name: access control
nodes.summary: access control — subclass of of Role-based access control.

nodes.name: Identity and access management
nodes.summary: Identity and access management or Identity management (IdM) is a framework of policies and technologies to ensure that the right users have the appropriate access to technology resources (Wikipedia)

nodes.name: DevOps
nodes.summary: DevOps is the integration and automation of software development and information technology operations (Wikipedia)

nodes.name: software development methodology
nodes.summary: software development methodology — subclass of of DevOps.

nodes.name: Modern AI stack
nodes.summary: Prompt, evaluate, and deploy LLMs at scale.

nodes.name: Large language model
nodes.summary: A large language model (LLM) is an AI Model trained on a vast amount of text for natural language processing tasks, especially language generation (Wikipedia)

nodes.name: Retrieval-augmented generation
nodes.summary: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) is a technique that enables large language models (LLMs) to retrieve and incorporate new information from external data sources (Wikipedia)

nodes.name: Machine learning
nodes.summary: Machine learning (ML) is a field of study in artificial intelligence concerned with the development and study of statistical algorithms that can learn from data and generalize to unseen data, and thus perform tasks witho (Wikipedia)

nodes.name: Prompt engineering
nodes.summary: Prompt engineering is the process of structuring natural language inputs to produce specified outputs from a generative artificial intelligence (GenAI) model (Wikipedia)

nodes.name: Agentic system design
nodes.summary: Build tool-using agents, sub-agents, MCP servers, and agent skills.

nodes.name: Intelligent agent
nodes.summary: In artificial intelligence, an intelligent agent is an entity that perceives its environment, takes actions autonomously to achieve goals, and may improve its performance through machine learning or by acquiring knowledg (Wikipedia)

nodes.name: Software agent
nodes.summary: In computer science, a software agent is a computer program that acts for a user or another program in a relationship of agency. (Wikipedia)

nodes.name: Model Context Protocol
nodes.summary: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is an open standard and open-source framework introduced by Anthropic in November 2024 to standardize the way artificial intelligence (AI) systems like large language models (LLMs) integr (Wikipedia)

nodes.name: The FDE craft
nodes.summary: Own ambiguity: embed with the customer, scope the smallest win, codify patterns.

nodes.name: Solution architecture
nodes.summary: Solution architecture is a term used in information technology with various definitions, such as "a description of a discrete and focused business operation or activity and how IS/IT supports that operation". (Wikipedia)

nodes.name: Requirements analysis
nodes.summary: In systems engineering and software engineering, requirements analysis focuses on the tasks that determine the needs or conditions to meet the new or altered product or project, taking account of the possibly conflicting (Wikipedia)

nodes.name: Technical leadership & playbooks
nodes.summary: Represent at the highest level; sequence delivery; codify playbooks.

nodes.name: Engineering management
nodes.summary: Engineering management is the application of engineering methods, tools, and techniques to business management systems (Wikipedia)

nodes.name: Language
nodes.summary: Language is a structured system of communication that consists of grammar and vocabulary (Wikipedia)

nodes.name: python-ai-agent-frameworks-demos
nodes.summary: A repository of examples using Python AI Agent frameworks that work with GitHub Models and Azure OpenAI. (GitHub)

nodes.name: vector-graph-db
nodes.summary: Examples of Vector  DBs (GitHub)

nodes.name: emergency operations
nodes.summary: task-oriented activity of task forces (Web)

nodes.name: fintech-agent-observability-evaluation
nodes.summary: (GitHub)

nodes.name: Mali
nodes.summary: country in West Africa (Web)

nodes.name: Pattern
nodes.summary: A pattern is a regularity in the world, in human-made design, or in abstract ideas (Wikipedia)

skills.name: Production SWE
skills.oneLine: Ship production backend/services in Python (and a second language).
notGoodAt: greenfield research with no delivery pressure

skills.name: Enterprise / hybrid deployment
skills.oneLine: Deploy inside the customer's environment under real access/security constraints.
notGoodAt: pure SaaS with no on-prem / VPC reality

skills.name: Modern AI stack
skills.oneLine: Prompt, evaluate, and deploy LLMs at scale.

skills.name: Agentic system design
skills.oneLine: Build tool-using agents, sub-agents, MCP servers, and agent skills.

skills.name: The FDE craft
skills.oneLine: Own ambiguity: embed with the customer, scope the smallest win, codify patterns.
notGoodAt: fully-specced ticket work handed to you in pieces

skills.name: Technical leadership & playbooks
skills.oneLine: Represent at the highest level; sequence delivery; codify playbooks.
notGoodAt: deep IC-only crunch with no stakeholder surface
